body.genesis_faq: Is public genesis and merkle data OK? Yes. Genesis block hash, merkle root, timestamp, nonce, and bits are public consensus parameters — every HOBC node uses them to validate the chain. Bitcoin and other UTXO networks publish the same data. This is not a private key, wallet seed, or RPC credential.
body.p1: Values below match HOBC mainnet consensus in the published node source. Block height 1 is the documented launch reserve block with a separate subsidy rule.
body.p10: Recommended mainnet peers for initial sync. Additional DNS seeds may be added as network infrastructure expands.
body.p11: Listing, integration, and due diligence inquiries:
body.p12: Brand assets and official PDF documents for exchange media review and due diligence.
body.p2: Burn tracking is published for supply transparency. Burns are documented as permanent supply reduction events, not as a price-increase mechanism.
body.p3: Subsidy rules are enforced by node consensus. This site does not estimate future market value from emission or burn activity.
body.p4: SHA256 solo mining, worker naming, pool URLs, shares, and payout rules for home miners.
body.p5: Linux node setup, ports, peers, sync checks, and safe RPC configuration.
body.p6: Mainnet, testnet, and regtest port reference for integrations and firewall planning.
body.p7: Operator commands for hobbyhashd and hobbyhash-cli. RPC must remain local/private.
body.p8: Social channels are listed for verification only. URLs marked “To be confirmed” will be updated when accounts are finalized.
body.p9: Highlighted checksums for current recommended releases. Full checksum list files are linked for all published builds.
body.reserve_intro: HOBC uses a transparent {percent} launch reserve (8,400,000 HOBC) assigned at block height 1. The reserve supports ecosystem operations such as infrastructure, listings, miner growth programs, and community support under a published allocation policy.

hero.title: HOBC Exchange Listing Packet
list.reserve.1: Reserve wallet and outgoing transactions are tracked on the public Launch Reserve page.
list.reserve.2: Reserve spends can include public admin descriptions for operational transparency.
list.reserve.3: Reserve policy is described as transparent reserve management and supply planning — not profit promises.
list.reserve.4: Burn events, when published, are tracked separately on the Burn Tracker page.
list.security.1: Download only from {website} and verify SHA256 checksums before installing node or wallet software.
list.security.2: Mainnet RPC (port {rpc_port}) must stay on loopback or a trusted private network. Do not expose RPC to the public internet.
list.security.3: The public web wallet is custodial. HOBC also offers an official Windows standalone wallet on the downloads page. Long-term holdings should use the Windows standalone wallet or other local wallet software with encrypted backups.
list.security.4: Reserve and burn pages use live chain or wallet data where available; unavailable values remain labeled honestly.
list.security.5: Third-party security audit status: To be confirmed. Audit scope and reports will be linked here when published.
list.security.6: See the Security Guide for checksum verification, backup practices, and scam avoidance.
